purpose: Discriminative pre-dispatch validation of plan+contract for ruby-backtick-extraction-v1. Smallest of 3 follow-on milestones (~25 source LOC + 2 fixtures + 2 tests). Plan adds a localized dispatch arm in detect_sinks_ast keyed on `language == Ruby && descendant.kind() == "subshell"` to close 1 carry-forward (ruby_command_injection_positive) without touching ast_utils.rs.
verdict_summary: Plan is structurally sound but contains 1 BLOCKER-class load-bearing falsehood (E1: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast does NOT have a 'generic descend-named-children body' that would walk subshell→interpolation→identifier) and 1 BLOCKER-class compilation hazard (E2: pseudo-code in plan §5 omits the `tainted: bool` field required by TaintSink struct). Both are fixable inside M2's existing LOC envelope without re-planning. Plan's R1 risk register actually captures E1 as MEDIUM with the correct mitigation (extend helper with Ruby-specific subshell descent arm) — the discriminative finding is that R1 is NOT optional contingency; it is REQUIRED. Remaining design (Option B, no call_node_kinds extension, no RUBY_AST_SINKS entry, FP regression-guard preserved) is verified correct.

premortem_metadata.discriminative_pattern: 5/5 prior premortems caught BLOCKER-class issues; this run continues the streak.

claims_verified_by_grep: Single occurrence of `for pattern in patterns.sinks` in taint.rs (line 4275) — confirms no parallel build_sinks_ast_index loop (R3 well-bounded)
All 3 occurrences of `"subshell"` in tree-sitter-ruby node-types.json — single named-node kind, no separate %x form
claims_NOT_independently_verified: M1 RED capture output (test fails at HEAD) — not run; trusted from carry-forward documentation
Empirical tree-sitter-ruby parse of `\`#{cmd}\`` and `%x{#{cmd}}` — not run; trusted from grammar node-types.json static analysis
cargo test --workspace post-M2 GREEN status — cannot pre-verify pre-implementation

C1_extract_call_name_ruby_only_matches_c.claim: extract_call_name_ruby at ast_utils.rs:707-728 matches only `"call" | "method_call"` and returns None for subshell.
C1_extract_call_name_ruby_only_matches_c.evidence: ast_utils.rs:708-728 — match arm is exactly `match node.kind() { "call" | "method_call" => …, _ => None }`. Confirms detect_sinks_ast call_names path AND the W2-pre call-shape path in member_patterns_match BOTH gate on extract_call_name(...).is_some(), so subshell cannot be matched via the for-pattern-in-patterns.sinks loop.

C3_tree_sitter_ruby_subshell_collapses_b.claim: tree-sitter-ruby 0.23.1 parses both `\`cmd\`` and `%x{cmd}` as a single `subshell` named-node kind with children {escape_sequence, interpolation, string_content}.
C3_tree_sitter_ruby_subshell_collapses_b.evidence: node-types.json:3194-3216 — single subshell type definition with exactly those 3 child types. Three occurrences of "subshell" total: lines 333, 497 (subtype lists in supertypes _arg/_primary), and 3195 (definition). No %x-prefixed node kind exists. Single dispatch arm covers ALL 6 lexical forms (`...`, %x{...}, %x[...], %x(...), and 2 less-common variants).

C4_string_node_kinds_ruby_excludes_subsh.claim: string_node_kinds(Ruby) = ["string", "string_content"] at ast_utils.rs:48; subshell is not in the slice.
C4_string_node_kinds_ruby_excludes_subsh.evidence: ast_utils.rs:48 — exactly `Language::Ruby => &["string", "string_content"]`. is_in_string walking up from a subshell node: subshell.kind != string && != string_content → not filtered. The dispatch arm CAN fire on the subshell node itself. (Inner string_content child IS filtered, but that is irrelevant since the arm keys on subshell, not on its children.)

C5_no_parallel_build_sinks_ast_index.claim: Sinks dispatch flows through detect_sinks_ast exclusively at HEAD (R3 well-bounded).
C5_no_parallel_build_sinks_ast_index.evidence: grep -n 'for pattern in patterns.sinks' taint.rs returns exactly one hit at line 4275 (inside detect_sinks_ast). No build_sinks_ast_index symbol. R3 stands; M3 binary-smoke remains the right gate.

C6_FP_fixture_has_no_real_subshell.claim: command_injection_string_literal_fp.rb mentions WORD 'backtick cmd' inside a string but has no real `…` subshell node.
C6_FP_fixture_has_no_real_subshell.evidence: Read full fixture (11 lines). Line 8 contains `"render html_safe; backtick cmd; File.open(p); Net::HTTP.get(u); YAML.load(d)"` — entire string is a `string` literal with `string_content` child. No backtick-pair anywhere. Descendants-walk yields zero subshell nodes; dispatch arm cannot fire on this fixture. FP regression-guard preserved.

C7_existing_positive_fixture_uses_backti.claim: ruby/command_injection_positive.rb uses `\`#{cmd}\`` shape with assignment cmd = params[:cmd] on prior line.
C7_existing_positive_fixture_uses_backti.evidence: Read full fixture (9 lines). Line 6: `cmd = params[:cmd]`. Line 7: `\`#{cmd}\``. Confirms primary closure target shape. Source detection on line 6 (params[ raw-fallback). Sink line 7 currently emits no sink → test RED at HEAD.

C8_TaintSink_struct_requires_tainted_boo.claim: PLAN-IMPLICIT: TaintSink struct construction omitted in §5 pseudo-code.
C8_TaintSink_struct_requires_tainted_boo.verdict: FALSIFIED — plan §5 + dispatch-contract M2 additions both omit `tainted: false`
C8_TaintSink_struct_requires_tainted_boo.evidence: taint.rs:208-222 — TaintSink has 5 fields: var, line, sink_type, tainted: bool, statement: Option<String>. Existing construction at line 4341 includes `tainted: false`. Plan §5 line 4339-4347 pseudo-code omits the field — would not compile under -D warnings. Sub-blocker — fixable at implementation time by mirroring the existing site (line 4341), but kraken must NOT copy the plan's snippet verbatim.

C9_extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_has_.claim: PLAN §0 lines 86-89 + investigation.json `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_audit` claim 'GENERIC PATH walks named descendants of the input node looking for an identifier' for subshell.
C9_extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_has_.verdict: FALSIFIED — there is NO such generic descendants walk for non-PHP/non-OCaml inputs
C9_extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_has_.evidence: taint.rs:3934-4065 (full body read). Structure: (1) PHP-specific BFS arm for echo_statement/print_intrinsic at L3948-3983. (2) OCaml-specific arm for application_expression at L3989-4016. (3) Generic path L4022-4036: requires `child_by_field_name("arguments")` OR a child whose kind contains 'argument' or equals 'call_suffix'. subshell has NEITHER an `arguments` field NOR any child whose kind contains 'argument' (children are interpolation/string_content/escape_sequence). The `?` operator at L4036 returns None for subshell. (4) The args-list walk at L4039-4062 is then unreachable for subshell. CONCLUSION: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast(subshell, source, Ruby) RETURNS None. The plan's narrative §0 is wrong; the investigation.json `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_audit.verdict_for_subshell` is wrong. The plan's R1 risk register IS correct that this is MEDIUM — but the contingency 'M2 must extend the helper' is REQUIRED, not optional. Without the extension, the dispatch arm extracts no var → no sink emitted → fixture stays RED → M2 fails its stop_threshold.

C10_var_extraction_chain_secondary_helpe.claim: DERIVED: extract_assignment_rhs_ident(line 7 stmt_text="\`#{cmd}\`") and extract_source_var_from_statement(stmt_text) both return None for the bare subshell line.
C10_var_extraction_chain_secondary_helpe.evidence: Line 7 of fixture is exactly `\`#{cmd}\`` — no `=` token, so extract_assignment_rhs_ident's last-`=` search at L4087-4131 returns None. extract_source_var_from_statement at L846-880 looks for `>>`, `&`, or constructor-style `Type var(args)` — none match a backtick subshell. Three-helper chain in plan §5 (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast || extract_assignment_rhs_ident || extract_source_var_from_statement) returns None for the canonical fixture. WITHOUT the R1 helper extension, dispatch arm emits no sink. Confirms C9's blocking impact.

C11_RUBY_AST_SINKS_unaffected.claim: No new RUBY_AST_SINKS entry needed; subshell is non-call-shaped so cannot match any AstSinkPattern via existing structural paths.
C11_RUBY_AST_SINKS_unaffected.evidence: member_patterns_match at L3850-3919 has 3 paths: (1) member_access_expression structural — subshell has no field-access shape; (2) call-shape via dotted call name at L3886-3906 — gates on call_node_kinds.contains(subshell)==false; (3) raw-substring fallback at L3913-3917 — only fires if pattern.pat_rcv.is_empty() AND descendant_text.contains(pat_field). No existing RUBY_AST_SINKS entry has empty pat_rcv with a subshell-friendly pat_field. Adding such an entry would FP on backtick chars in source; correctly rejected. Plan's claim verified.

C12_FP_regression_guard_safety.claim: Existing ruby_command_injection_string_literal_fp will remain GREEN; new %x{} FP fixture is also safe.
C12_FP_regression_guard_safety.evidence: C6 establishes existing FP has no subshell node. New FP fixture (plan §4) is `s = "use %x{cmd} for inline shell"` — `%x{cmd}` inside a `string` literal does NOT parse as subshell; the `"..."` parses as `string` containing `string_content` whose text happens to contain the characters '%', 'x', '{', etc. Tree-sitter-ruby tokenizes `%x{...}` as subshell ONLY at expression position, not inside a string literal. Descendants-walk yields zero subshell nodes for the new FP fixture; dispatch arm cannot fire. ZERO FP regression.

C13_GREEN_regression_risk.claim: DERIVED: dispatch arm gated on `language == Ruby && descendant.kind() == "subshell"` cannot affect tests in other languages or non-subshell Ruby tests.
C13_GREEN_regression_risk.evidence: Strict gate on language enum + exact kind string match. Cannot fire on non-Ruby AST. For Ruby tests without backtick/%x{} (e.g., rr_baseline_per_language_test, rr_module_function_integ_test, ruby_xss_*, ruby_sql_injection_*), descendants-walk yields zero subshell nodes — arm doesn't fire — no behavioral change. Other 158 currently-GREEN tests in vuln_migration_v1_red are unaffected unless they use backtick/%x{} shape, and per investigation.json side_effect_audit they don't.

C14_atomic_M2_LOC_bound.claim: M2 atomic commit ships within ~60 LOC including dispatch arm + 2 fixtures + 2 tests.
C14_atomic_M2_LOC_bound.evidence: Plan estimates ~25 LOC for dispatch arm. Forced R1 contingency (helper extension) bumps source LOC to ~40 + dispatch arm ~25 = ~65 source LOC. With fixtures (~14) + tests (~20), total ~99 LOC — exceeds the contract's loc_delta:60 estimate by ~39 LOC. NOT a blocker (contract says 'estimate', not hard cap), but kraken should be aware that the R1 path is the EXPECTED path, not the unlikely contingency.

verified_correct: Plan's tree-sitter-ruby node-kind audit (C3, C7, C12)
Plan's call_node_kinds / extract_call_name_ruby non-extension rationale (C1, C2)
Plan's FP regression-guard analysis for both existing and new FP fixtures (C6, C12)
Plan's side-effect audit (sources/sanitizers/references unaffected) — verified via call_node_kinds invariance (C2)
verified_falsified: Plan §0 + investigation.json claim 'extract_first_identifier_arg_ast generic body picks up the identifier' — FALSE; helper returns None for subshell because it requires an `arguments` field or 'argument'-named child (C9). R1 contingency is REQUIRED, not optional.
Plan §5 pseudo-code omits the `tainted: bool` field that TaintSink struct requires — would not compile under `-D warnings` (C8).
verification_unfilled: M1 must produce the empirical RED capture (asserted, trusted)

M1 must produce empirical extract_first_identifier_arg_ast verdict — plan's M1 gate already requires this and is the catch for C9 IFF M1 is run faithfully and its 'helper_returns_none' branch is taken seriously

tigers.title: Plan's narrative misrepresents extract_first_identifier_arg_ast as having a generic descend-named-children body
tigers.details: The plan §0 (lines 82-89) and investigation.json `extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_audit.verdict_for_subshell` BOTH claim the helper has a generic body that walks named descendants. It does NOT. The function has PHP-specific arm (echo/print) at L3948-3983, OCaml-specific arm at L3989-4016, then a strict 'find arguments-list child' path at L4022-4036 that returns None via `?` for any node lacking an `arguments` field or 'argument'-kinded child. subshell has NEITHER. Without the R1 helper extension, the dispatch arm extracts var=None and emits zero sinks → ruby_command_injection_positive STAYS RED → M2 stop_threshold fails → atomic commit must be reverted per rollback_rule.
tigers.remediation: Treat plan's R1 risk as REQUIRED, not contingent. M2 MUST include a Ruby-specific subshell descent arm in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (at the same site as PHP echo_statement / OCaml application_expression arms). The arm should: (1) check `language == Ruby && descendant.kind() == "subshell"`, (2) BFS over named children of subshell, recursing into `interpolation` nodes, (3) on finding the first `identifier` child, return its node_text. Or equivalently: extend the chain in detect_sinks_ast's NEW dispatch arm with a 4th fallback that does the BFS inline. Either is fine; pick whichever yields fewer total LOC. Updated source LOC estimate: ~40 (dispatch arm ~20 + helper extension ~20) instead of ~25. Total M2 LOC ~75-85.

tigers.title: Plan §5 pseudo-code omits required `tainted: bool` field — would fail `cargo clippy -D warnings`
tigers.details: TaintSink struct (taint.rs:210-222) has 5 fields: var, line, sink_type, tainted: bool, statement: Option<String>. Plan §5 line 4339-4347 pseudo-code shows 4 fields, missing `tainted`. The plan also references it as 'TaintSink { sink_type: ShellExec, var: <inner-ident>, ... }' — the `...` could be read as 'fill in the rest', but kraken following the literal snippet would produce a `error[E0063]: missing field` compile error. The existing TaintSink construction at L4341-4347 includes `tainted: false`.
tigers.remediation: Implementation-time fix: kraken MUST add `tainted: false` to the new TaintSink construction, mirroring L4341. Trivial. No plan edit needed if kraken is instructed to read the existing site rather than copy-paste plan §5.

elephants.title: M2 LOC bound likely exceeded by ~30-40 LOC due to forced R1 helper extension
elephants.details: Plan §1 says ~10-20 LOC source; contract says loc_delta:60 total. Forced R1 path raises source LOC to ~40 (dispatch arm ~20 + helper extension ~20), total to ~75-85 LOC. NOT a hard blocker — contract loc_delta is an estimate, and atomic_commit semantics permit overshooting if the gates pass. But kraken should plan for the larger envelope to avoid mid-implementation re-scoping.
elephants.remediation: Inform kraken the helper extension is the EXPECTED path; budget ~75 LOC for M2 atomic commit. Plan/contract do NOT need editing for this — the LOC estimate is non-binding.

elephants.title: M3 binary-smoke gate requires `tldr` binary path-resolved to local build
elephants.details: User's CLAUDE.md says `tldr` is on PATH symlinked from `~/.local/bin/tldr`. If that symlink points to an older release without the new dispatch arm, the binary-smoke gate at M3 would fail with a confusing 'no findings' message even though cargo test was GREEN. Documentation suggests using the PATH binary directly.
elephants.remediation: M3 should explicitly invoke `cargo run -p tldr-cli --release -- vuln <fixture> --lang ruby --format json` rather than `tldr vuln ...` to guarantee the locally-built binary is used. Or rebuild and re-symlink before M3. Plan can be tightened in M3 phase without re-issuing the dispatch contract.

elephants.title: Possibility that `is_in_string` filters the inner identifier `cmd` inside `\`#{cmd}\`` (relevant only IF helper extension recurses into interpolation)
elephants.details: is_in_string walks UP the parent chain from a node. For inner identifier `cmd`: identifier → interpolation → subshell → method → ... None of those kinds are in `["string", "string_content"]`. So is_in_string returns false for the inner identifier. Helper extension can safely descend without is_in_string blocking. (Note: the inner `string_content` CHILD of subshell IS filtered if visited as a descendant — but we don't visit it from the helper extension's BFS, only `interpolation` children.)
elephants.remediation: None required; flagged for clarity. Helper-extension BFS should explicitly recurse only into `interpolation` named-children (not all named children), preserving the is_in_string skip semantics by construction.

elephants.title: Plan-implicit assumption that NEW %x{} FP fixture's `"use %x{cmd} for inline shell"` parses without subshell
elephants.details: tree-sitter-ruby tokenizes `%x{...}` as subshell only at expression position, not inside a string literal. The fixture pattern `s = "use %x{cmd} for inline shell"` — the entire `"..."` is a string, and inside the string `%x{cmd}` is just characters of string_content, not a subshell. This is the same shape as the existing `command_injection_string_literal_fp.rb`'s 'backtick cmd' inside a string. The parsing is correct, but a tightening recommendation: M1 node-audit should explicitly assert this for the new fixture (parse the fixture, count subshell nodes, expect 0).
elephants.remediation: M1 node-audit should add a 4th assertion: parsing `s = "use %x{cmd} for inline shell"` yields zero subshell descendants. Trivial extension to the M1-node-audit.json schema.

elephants.title: Plan's stop_threshold cargo test count math: '161/168 GREEN' assumes new FP test passes
elephants.details: Stop_threshold uses 'or 159/166 if measured against original suite size'. Both metrics are correct, but the dual phrasing in plan.md/contract creates ambiguity for the verifier. Recommend M3-final-report.json explicitly state both numbers separately.

carry_forward_cap_check.predecessor_carry_forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket A Ruby (1 test: ruby_command_injection_positive)
carry_forward_cap_check.this_milestone_adds_to_carry_forward: 0 (no new RED tests authored that fail post-M2)
carry_forward_cap_check.verdict: WITHIN BOUND — milestone consumes 1 carry-forward unit and adds 0 new ones, NET -1.

remediations_required_before_dispatch.instruction: When dispatching M2 to kraken, EXPLICITLY instruct: 'extract_first_identifier_arg_ast does NOT have a generic descendants walk; for subshell input, the function's args-list-child path returns None. You MUST extend the function with a Ruby-specific arm (or do the BFS inline in the dispatch arm itself). The plan's R1 contingency is the EXPECTED path. Helper extension shape: BFS the subshell node's `interpolation` named-children, recurse, return first `identifier` text via node_text. Mirror the PHP echo_statement BFS at taint.rs:3954-3982 stylistically.'

When dispatching M2 to kraken, EXPLICITLY instruct: 'TaintSink construction REQUIRES the field `tainted: false` (5 fields total: var, line, sink_type, tainted, statement). Read taint.rs:4341-4347 for the canonical construction site BEFORE writing your dispatch-arm body; do NOT copy plan §5 verbatim — it omits this field.'
Inform kraken the LOC bound is now ~75-85 total (not 60). Loc_delta in dispatch-contract is non-binding; the atomic-commit gates (cargo check, clippy, all targeted tests GREEN) are the hard gates.
M3 binary-smoke MUST use `cargo run -p tldr-cli --release -- vuln ...` rather than `tldr vuln ...` from PATH. Document in M3-binary-smoke.json explicitly which binary was invoked.
M1 node-audit add 4th assertion: parsing the new %x{} FP fixture yields zero subshell descendants. Trivial; ensures the FP fixture design is sound BEFORE committing M2.

plan_strengths: Option B selection is empirically correct (verified C1, C2, C3, C5, C11)
FP regression-guard analysis correctly identifies that subshell-keyed dispatch cannot fire on string-only fixtures (verified C6, C12)
Side-effect audit (sources/sanitizers/references unaffected) is correctly rationalized via call_node_kinds invariance (verified C2)
Atomic-M2 + rollback-rule + binary-smoke-in-M3 architecture is sound
Plan correctly rejects Options A and A+ with cross-cutting-impact rationale
Plan correctly identifies the R1 risk and mitigation (BUT under-states it as 'contingent' rather than 'required')
plan_weaknesses: T1: misrepresents extract_first_identifier_arg_ast's structure (BLOCKER if M1 audit is skipped or misread)
T2: pseudo-code omits required TaintSink field (BLOCKER on cargo build)
E1: LOC estimate is optimistic by ~30-40 LOC due to forced R1 path

Plan §5's choice of var-extraction fallback chain (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast || extract_assignment_rhs_ident || extract_source_var_from_statement) — all THREE return None for the canonical fixture without the helper extension. The chain provides no marginal value over the helper extension alone for this milestone.
additional_observations.discriminative_pattern_continued: 5/5 prior premortems caught BLOCKERs; this is 6/6. T1+T2 are independently sufficient to fail the M2 atomic commit if the dispatch is launched with the plan's literal text. Both are fixable in-flight without re-planning.

go_no_go.rationale: Plan's design (Option B, no cross-cutting changes, FP-safe, atomic-M2) is structurally correct. Two BLOCKER-class issues exist (T1 helper-doesn't-descend, T2 missing tainted field) but both are fixable inside M2 without re-planning. Inform kraken of these explicitly in the dispatch prompt and the milestone proceeds. Plan's M1 gate is the catch for T1 if executed faithfully — but better to surface it now in the dispatch prompt rather than rely on M1 to discover it.
go_no_go.next_action: Dispatch M1 (RED capture + node-audit + helper audit) with explicit instruction that the helper audit MUST read taint.rs:3934-4065 in full and report the verdict for subshell input — NOT trust the plan's narrative. M2 dispatch waits on M1 verdict + the 2 blocking remediations.
